title: Egome Fass

{{Quote|You're getting decrepit; two tries to put away a bum like that!|Han Solo to Chewbacca, after the latter's defeat of Egome Fass|<ref name="hll" />}}
'''Egome Fass''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] who worked as an enforcer for a criminal [[J'uoch's mining camp|mining outfit]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After getting involved with Houk-[[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] gang conflicts on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Fass signed up as muscle for the twins [[J'uoch]] and [[R'all]], who had designs on the ancient treasure of the ''[[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|Queen of Ranroon]]''. In their pursuit they came into conflict with another group of treasure hunters that included the hulking [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], with whom Fass tangled multiple times. Although Fass got the better of Chewbacca in their first encounter, the Wookiee overpowered the Houk the second time around, crushing him to [[Death/Legends|death]] with a powerful bearhug.

==Biography==
{{Quote|They have a partner, Egome Fass, their enforcer. He's a big, mean humanoid, a Houk, even taller than Chewie there. All three came up the hard way, and that's how they play.|"Trooper" Badure|<ref name="hll" />}}
Born in [[45 BBY/Legends|45 BBY]],<ref name="hnn" /> Egome Fass was a member of the [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="hll" /> a race well-known for its size and toughness—<ref name="uaa">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> Fass, who had a hard and violent upbringing, stood out even among Houks for his large stature and mean temper.<ref name="hll" /> By the era of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] Fass had left his home and immigrated to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he became involved in local Houk gang warfare. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] Fass and a number of other Houks were a part of a large brawl with a [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] gang in [[Eastport/Legends|Eastport]], resulting in Fass' arrest. Fass was arraigned the next month along with several other offenders in [[Judicial Hearing Chamber Y-76]] in the [[Judicial Arcology/Legends|Judicial Arcology]] building, where Fass and a Weequay named [[Ker-Zakk]] overpowered [[Bailiff/Legends|bailiffs]] and began the fight anew. Security shortly [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] the two combatants into unconsciousness, and afterward, presiding [[Judicial/Legends|Judicial]] Officer [[Warren Sloat]] ordered an extended recess.<ref name="hnn" />

[[File:ChewbaccaCSWE.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Chewbacca, the Wookiee who ultimately proved to be Egome Fass's match.]]
By [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] Fass had found his way to the backwater [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]], where he found work as an enforcer for a pair of twins, [[J'uoch]] and [[R'all]], who ran an illegal [[J'uoch's mining camp|mining outfit]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], their small-time criminal enterprise learned of a much larger prize than just unlawful [[Mining/Legends|mining]]: they discovered that a group of former miners on Dellalt, [[Alexsandr Badure|Alexsandr "Trooper" Badure]] and sisters [[Lanni Troujow|Lanni]] and [[Hasti Troujow]], had somehow stumbled across the [[log-recorder]] of the ''[[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|Queen of Ranroon]]'', the fabled lost [[treasure ship]] of the ancient [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] despot [[Xim/Legends|Xim]]. Believing that the recorder would point them in the treasure's direction, Fass and the twins confronted them when they returned to Dellalt, having left to find offworld help in finding the ship. A frenzied shootout ensued in the streets outside [[Treasure Vaults of Xim the Despot|Xim's treasure vaults]], where Fass tangled for the first time with the other group's heavy muscle, a hulking [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] named [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. Fass attacked Chewbacca from behind and gained the physical advantage, although the Wookiee's partner [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] stopped Fass from striking a decisive blow with a stream of [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugs]] that bounced off Fass' body armor. Ultimately, the battle ended when Badure's group was forced to flee into the wilderness, with Fass and the twins stealing their [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', for good measure.<ref name="hll" />

Fass hadn't seen the last of Chewbacca or his cohorts, however. Several weeks later, the Wookiee and his partners found their way into the mining camp, their arrival coinciding with an attack from a [[Guardian Corps|battalion]] of ancient [[war-robot]]s led by the [[Survivors (Dellalt)|Survivors]], descendants of Xim's honor guard that had previously led an obscure, isolated existence in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]]. Fass and Chewbacca tangled again within the chaos of the battle, the two massive [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] fighting hand-to-hand amidst the crossfire of dozens of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Laser/Legends|bolts]]. Fass managed to get the Wookiee into a chokehold, only for Chewbacca to overpower the Houk and catch him in a crushing bearhug that ultimately cracked Fass's spine and [[Death/Legends|killed]] him.<ref name="hll" />

==Personality and traits==
Egome Fass was tough and violent from his early years—after getting involved with Houk gang warfare in his 20s,<ref name="hnn" /> Fass made a living out of physical intimidation, earning a reputation for his size and temper. Even taller than the fearsome Wookiee Chewbacca, the imposing Houk had no qualms fighting dirty, but Chewbacca ultimately proved to be his match with his uncommon strength and fighting spirit.<ref name="hll" />

==Behind the scenes==
Egome Fass first appeared in ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1980]].<ref name="hnn" />
